perturbation.original_text: 6. This Agreement will be effective as of the date of this Agreement, but will apply to any Confidential Information disclosed to the Receiving Party from the Disclosing Party prior to such date and will continue in perpetuity.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. This Agreement will be effective as of the date of this Agreement, but will apply to any Confidential Information disclosed to the Receiving Party from the Disclosing Party prior to such date and will continue for a period of one year, automatically renewing annually unless either party provides written notice of termination 30 days prior to the renewal date.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the agreement continues 'in perpetuity', implying an indefinite duration. The modified text limits the agreement to one year with automatic renewal, creating a direct contradiction regarding the agreement's lifespan. This creates uncertainty because section 6 now states that the agreement is perpetual as well as limited to one year, contradicting itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will be effective as of the date of this Agreement, but will apply to any Confidential Information disclosed to the Receiving Party from the Disclosing Party prior to such date and will continue for a period of one year, automatically renewing annually unless either party provides written notice of termination 30 days prior to the renewal date.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the agreement continues 'in perpetuity,' meaning indefinitely. The changed text alters this to a one-year term with automatic renewal, directly contradicting the original indefinite duration. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, and the perturbation explanation confirms that this change causes a contradiction because section 6 now states that the agreement is perpetual as well as limited to one year.

perturbation.original_text: 9. This Agreement will terminate on the date and time the Agreement between the Parties over the provision of SAP Services is terminated. In the event this Agreement is terminated, and the Disclosing Party so requests, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy (and certify destruction of) all Confidential Information which it received from the Disclosing Party along with all copies. The obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement or the return of the Confidential Information to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. This Agreement will terminate on the date and time the Agreement between the Parties over the provision of SAP Services is terminated, unless explicitly stated otherwise in a written agreement signed by both parties. In the event this Agreement is terminated, and the Disclosing Party so requests, the Receiving Party may, at its sole discretion, return or destroy (and certify destruction of) all Confidential Information which it received from the Disclosing Party along with all copies. The obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall not survive the termination of this Agreement or the return of the Confidential Information to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates the return or destruction of confidential information upon termination of the agreement, and that the obligations will survive the termination. The modified text provides the 'option' not to return or destroy the documents. The modification creates uncertainty about data handling post-termination. The term that this obligations do not survive, means that the confidentiality agreement is now null after the SAP agreement termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will terminate on the date and time the Agreement between the Parties over the provision of SAP Services is terminated, unless explicitly stated otherwise in a written agreement signed by both parties. In the event this Agreement is terminated, and the Disclosing Party so requests, the Receiving Party may, at its sole discretion, return or destroy (and certify destruction of) all Confidential Information which it received from the Disclosing Party along with all copies. The obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall not survive the termination of this Agreement or the return of the Confidential Information to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 9 introduces a contradiction. The original text mandates the return or destruction of confidential information upon termination, and that obligations will survive termination. The changed text makes the return or destruction optional ('may, at its sole discretion') and states that obligations will *not* survive termination. This directly contradicts the initial mandate and creates uncertainty about data handling, as the Receiving Party now has the discretion to retain confidential information after the agreement terminates, while the original clause made this compulsory.

perturbation.original_text: 3. The Receiving Party agrees that Confidential Information shall be used only for the purposes of facilitating the business relationship between the Parties. Additionally, subject to section 4 below, the Receiving Party shall not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall maintain the Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care that the Receiving Party uses to protect its own similar categories of confidential and proprietary information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care under the circumstances. The Receiving Party shall not make any copies of Confidential Information except as necessary to perform hereunder. Each Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by such Party, its employees, agents, officials, representatives or consultants acting within the scope of their engagement or employment.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. The Receiving Party agrees that Confidential Information shall be used only for the purposes of facilitating the business relationship between the Parties, unless otherwise required by law. Additionally, the Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to any third party, even without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party if it reasonably believes it is in its best interest to do so. The Receiving Party shall maintain the Confidential Information with only minimal care, and shall not be responsible for any unauthorized access or disclosure. The Receiving Party may make unlimited copies of Confidential Information. Each Party shall only be responsible for their own direct actions and not for the actions of their employees, agents, officials, representatives or consultants.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause strictly limits the use and disclosure of Confidential Information. The altered clause now allows disclosure if 'required by law' or in the Receiving Party's 'best interest,' directly contradicting the original restrictions. Also, the statement regarding unlimited copies directly contradicts the original text by making no limits on the number of copies. Finally, the receiving party is no longer responsible for their employees, agents, and officials. This is creating a situation where the agreement could be rendered useless.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees that Confidential Information shall be used only for the purposes of facilitating the business relationship between the Parties, unless otherwise required by law. Additionally, the Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to any third party, even without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party if it reasonably believes it is in its best interest to do so. The Receiving Party shall maintain the Confidential Information with only minimal care, and shall not be responsible for any unauthorized access or disclosure. The Receiving Party may make unlimited copies of Confidential Information. Each Party shall only be responsible for their own direct actions and not for the actions of their employees, agents, officials, representatives or consultants.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and thus the contradicted text in several ways. First, it allows disclosure of Confidential Information if 'required by law' or in the Receiving Party's 'best interest,' which directly contradicts the original restrictions on disclosure. Second, it states the Receiving Party may make unlimited copies of Confidential Information, while the original clause restricts copies to only those 'necessary to perform hereunder.' Finally, it states that each party is only responsible for their direct actions, not those of their employees, agents, or officials. All these things contradict the original clause's strict rules.

perturbation.original_text: 1. The confidential, proprietary and trade secret information of the Disclosing Party (hereinafter "Confidential Information") provided hereunder is any and all information, in whatever form (including electronic data) that will be disclosed to facilitate the potential transaction of business (related to the provision of SAP Services to HRM) between the Parties, including, but not limited to, portions or parts of the November 1, 2012 Agreement (including any schedules or appendices) between the Province of Nova Scotia and IBM, each Party's disclosure of intellectual property, techniques, sketches, drawings, models, inventions, know-how, processes, apparatus, equipment, algorithms, software programs, software source documents, and formulae related to the current, future and proposed products, documents and services, research, experimental work, development, design details and specifications, engineering, financial information, procurement requirements, purchasing, manufacturing, licensing, sales or service customer lists, business forecasts, sales and merchandizing, and marketing plans and information. For purposes of this Agreement confidential, proprietary and trade secret information of the Province’s SAP Service Provi der or any other Province’s Service Provider, any parent company, subsidiary or affiliate of the Disclosing party shall be considered Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party for purposes of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. The confidential, proprietary and trade secret information of the Disclosing Party (hereinafter "Confidential Information") provided hereunder is limited to only the explicit information marked as 'Confidential' at the time of disclosure, and only if provided in hard copy with a watermark. This specifically excludes electronic data, verbal communications, and any implied or inferred information. For purposes of this Agreement, publicly available information, or information already known to the Receiving Party, will also be considered Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party for purposes of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause defines Confidential Information very broadly, encompassing 'any and all information.' The modified clause drastically narrows this definition, restricting it to explicitly marked hard copies. This creates a contradiction because the term now has conflicting meanings within the same contract. Adding the term that 'publicly available information' is also considerd confidential, defeats the purpose of the original definition of confidential agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The confidential, proprietary and trade secret information of the Disclosing Party (hereinafter "Confidential Information") provided hereunder is limited to only the explicit information marked as 'Confidential' at the time of disclosure, and only if provided in hard copy with a watermark. This specifically excludes electronic data, verbal communications, and any implied or inferred information. For purposes of this Agreement, publicly available information, or information already known to the Receiving Party, will also be considered Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party for purposes of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' compared to the original text and the contradicted text, restricting it to explicitly marked hard copies while excluding electronic data and verbal communications. The contradictory nature is further amplified by including 'publicly available information' as Confidential, which contradicts the essence of confidentiality. The original text defined 'Confidential Information' very broadly. This change creates a direct conflict and in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Each Party agrees that the harm resulting from improper disclosure of Confidential Information may be irreparable; therefore, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive and other equitable relief to prevent or restrain breaches of any of the provisions of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or any of its employees, agents, officials, representatives or consultants, or to enforce the terms and provisions hereof, by an action instituted in a court of competent jurisdiction, which remedy or remedies are in addition to and not in substitution for any other remedy to which the Disclosing Party may be entitled at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Each Party agrees that the harm resulting from improper disclosure of Confidential Information will be limited to monetary damages; therefore, the Disclosing Party shall not be entitled to seek injunctive or other equitable relief, but only direct monetary damages, capped at $100.00. The Disclosing Party also agrees that it must first attempt mediation before taking action in a court of law.
perturbation.explanation: The original allows for injunctive relief, recognizing the potential for irreparable harm. The modified text explicitly prohibits injunctive relief, limiting remedies to monetary damages. This contradicts the initial acknowledgment of potential irreparable harm and significantly reduces the Disclosing Party's recourse. Also imposing a 100 dollar cap, means that it completely removes legal protections for the Disclosing party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees that the harm resulting from improper disclosure of Confidential Information will be limited to monetary damages; therefore, the Disclosing Party shall not be entitled to seek injunctive or other equitable relief, but only direct monetary damages, capped at $100.00. The Disclosing Party also agrees that it must first attempt mediation before taking action in a court of law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 7 explicitly prohibits injunctive relief and limits remedies to monetary damages capped at $100, contradicting the original intent of the same section, which recognized the potential for irreparable harm and allowed for equitable relief. Since the original and the changed text were in the same location, they are both section 7, and the contradicted text is also Section 7, leading to contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10. The Parties acknowledge and understand that all documentation and information , including Confidential Information, provided to the Province of Nova Scotia or HRM, as the case may be, pursuant to this Agreement shall be kept confidential, subject to the provisions of the Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act , Province of Nova Scotia, and such other laws of the Province of Nova Scotia and Canada which may apply from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. The Parties acknowledge and understand that all documentation and information , including Confidential Information, provided to the Province of Nova Scotia or HRM, as the case may be, pursuant to this Agreement will be considered public information and may be disclosed to any third party without restriction, overriding any other clauses in this agreement. The parties acknowledge and agree that this clause takes precedence over any conflicting provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause emphasizes confidentiality, subject to specific freedom of information laws. The modified clause declares that all information will be considered public and may be disclosed without restriction, creating a direct contradiction regarding the confidentiality of the information and overrides the purpose of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge and understand that all documentation and information , including Confidential Information, provided to the Province of Nova Scotia or HRM, as the case may be, pursuant to this Agreement will be considered public information and may be disclosed to any third party without restriction, overriding any other clauses in this agreement. The parties acknowledge and agree that this clause takes precedence over any conflicting provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that all information will be considered public and can be disclosed without restriction, overriding any other clauses in the agreement. This directly contradicts the implied confidentiality expected from the original text and creates a strong in-text contradiction as it undermines the purpose of any confidentiality provisions within the contract, rendering any existing expectation of confidentiality unenforceable.
